query_strategy_description: 22 web-search queries grouped into 6 buckets that map to the 16-section consumer_segmentation structure. Use WebSearch (native Claude tool). Prefer national statistics bureaus, central-bank household surveys, consumer-panel providers with disclosed methodology (Kantar Worldpanel, NielsenIQ retail audit — read but never cite by name), listed-company investor decks with consumer KPIs, and academic consumer-behavior papers. Avoid named aggregators (anti-positioning rule).

localized_search.source_tagging: English alias only (e.g. [GSO 2024]) per L.3. Page-bottom SOURCE KEY may include original local-language source name for traceability.
localized_search.currency_hint: Fire a parallel local-currency query for sizing buckets (e.g. VND/IDR/THB/JPY/KRW) since local stats rarely report in USD.
localized_search.note: Phase M.1: dual-language search. EN baseline still runs (existing query_buckets above); this block governs the additional local-language pass.

query_buckets.purpose: Population demographic distribution by age × income × geography → drives 06_demographic + persona demo grids
queries: {{country}} household income distribution {{year}} consumer expenditure survey
{{country}} middle class size {{year}} household income tier
expected_sources: national statistics bureau (BPS, GSO, NSO, PSA, DOSM, SingStat, Statistics Korea, Statistics Bureau Japan)

query_buckets.purpose: Category-level consumer spending — frequency, basket size, share of wallet → drives 07_behavioral + persona behavior grids
queries: {{country}} household {{industry}} share of wallet basket size
expected_sources: listed-company quarterly investor decks (basket / frequency disclosures)
academic consumer-behavior papers with disclosed panel n
query_buckets.expected_tag_mix: secondary 60% / estimate 40% (frequency × basket cross-tabs often need KIRA triangulation)

query_buckets.purpose: Channel mix per consumer cohort — modern trade, traditional, e-comm, social commerce, direct → drives 12_channel_preferences + persona channel_pref_bar
queries: {{country}} live commerce TikTok Shopee Lazada {{industry}} share
expected_sources: e-commerce platform investor decks (Sea Group, Tokopedia, Coupang etc.)

query_buckets.purpose: NPS, brand-affinity, switching behavior → drives 14_brand_affinity + persona quote_callout + WTP wiring
expected_sources: consumer panel providers with methodology disclosure (read, do not cite by name)
query_buckets.expected_tag_mix: secondary 55% / estimate 35% / primary 10% (KIRA triangulates NPS from multi-source where panel data isn't named)

query_buckets.purpose: WTP curves, price points, elasticity → drives 13_wtp + persona WTP anchor
queries: {{country}} {{industry}} price points consumer willingness to pay {{year}}
query_buckets.expected_tag_mix: secondary 45% / estimate 50% / primary 5% (WTP is the section most likely to require KIRA estimate with anchor methodology)
query_buckets.note: WTP triangulation pattern: anchor on a published median price point × elasticity from a comparable category to back-solve persona WTP bands. Always disclose anchor in source line.

query_buckets.purpose: Values, lifestyle clusters, cultural anchors → drives 08_psychographic + persona headline + quote_callout authenticity
expected_sources: central bank household finance + sentiment surveys
industry consumer-trend reports with disclosed methodology
query_buckets.note: Voice rule: psychographic claims are the highest-risk for stereotype drift. Every values claim needs an explicit anchor (survey n, year, geography) or it gets downgraded to [estimate] with anchor disclosure.

query_execution_rules.parallelism: Run queries in parallel within a bucket (up to 5 concurrent). Sequential across buckets so later buckets can reference earlier findings — especially: persona codenames + headlines depend on psychographic cluster outputs which depend on demographic + behavioral anchors.
result_filtering: Discard results from named-firm aggregators (Mordor, Frost, Euromonitor, IMARC, Synovate, Ipsos, etc.) — anti-positioning rule
Prefer results with disclosed panel composition (n=, geography, year, recruitment method)
If a stat appears in 3+ independent sources within ±10%, treat as well-anchored secondary
If a stat appears in only 1 source, either find corroboration or downgrade to KIRA estimate with anchor commentary

For consumer quotes used in persona quote_callouts: must trace to a real published source, an IDI panel KIRA can credit by composition (e.g. 'IDI panel n=24, Jakarta + Surabaya, 2025'), or be flagged as [estimate] composite with anchor disclosure
query_execution_rules.estimate_methodology_template: Anchored estimate: {{anchor_metric}} × {{ratio}} = {{output}}. E.g. 'National household {{industry}} spend USD {{X}}/yr × Persona 02 income-tier ratio ~1.6x → Persona 02 WTP band USD {{Y}}-{{Z}}/purchase'.
query_execution_rules.persona_clustering_rule: Before drafting persona pages, run a clustering pass on demographic × behavioral × psychographic outputs to identify the 3 most-distinct, most-value-relevant clusters. Personas must differ on at least 3 of these 8 axes: age band, income tier, geography, frequency, basket size, channel mix, WTP band, dominant value. If two clusters collapse on too many axes, merge and pick a different third cluster.
query_execution_rules.research_data_output_format: Aggregate findings into a single JSON file shaped like references/research_data_R0152.json — top-level keys per bucket, each value tagged with source URL + access date + extraction method + panel composition where applicable.

template_variable_resolution.{{country_stats_bureau}}: Mapped via lookup: ID→BPS, VN→GSO, TH→NSO, PH→PSA, MY→DOSM, SG→SingStat, JP→Statistics Bureau Japan, KR→Statistics Korea (KOSIS)
template_variable_resolution.{{year}}: Topic year (parser output, default current year)
template_variable_resolution.{{persona_1_codename}}: Filled at content-gen time after clustering pass — short mono-uppercase tag + age band, e.g. 'URBAN VALUE-SEEKER · 28-34'
